2603.26013.title: Toward Culturally Grounded Natural Language Processing
2603.26013.paper_summary: Recent progress in multilingual NLP is often taken as evidence of broader global inclusivity, but a growing literature shows that multilingual capability and cultural competence come apart. This paper synthesizes over 50 papers from 2020--2026 spanning multilingual performance inequality, cross-lingual transfer, culture-aware evaluation, cultural alignment, multimodal local-knowledge modeling, benchmark design critiques, and community-grounded data practices. Across this literature, training data coverage remains a strong determinant of performance, yet it is not sufficient: tokenization, prompt language, translated benchmark design, culturally specific supervision, and multimodal context all materially affect outcomes. Recent work on Global-MMLU, CDEval, WorldValuesBench, CulturalBench, CULEMO, CulturalVQA, GIMMICK, DRISHTIKON, WorldCuisines, CARE, CLCA, and newer critiques of benchmark design and community-grounded evaluation shows that strong multilingual models can still flatten local norms, misread culturally grounded cues, and underperform in lower-resource or community-specific settings. We argue that the field should move from treating languages as isolated rows in a benchmark spreadsheet toward modeling communicative ecologies: the institutions, scripts, translation pipelines, domains, modalities, and communities through which language is used. On that basis, we propose a research agenda for culturally grounded NLP centered on richer contextual metadata, culturally stratified evaluation, participatory alignment, within-language variation, and multimodal community-aware design.
2603.26013.paper_summary_zh: 最近在多語言自然語言處理（NLP）方面的進展常被視為更廣泛全球包容性的證據，但越來越多的文獻顯示，多語言能力和文化能力是分開的。本文綜合了2020年至2026年間超過50篇論文，涵蓋了多語言表現不平等、跨語言轉移、文化意識評估、文化對齊、多模態本地知識建模、基準設計批評以及社區基礎數據實踐。在這些文獻中，訓練數據的覆蓋範圍仍然是表現的強大決定因素，但這並不充分：分詞、提示語言、翻譯的基準設計、文化特定的監督以及多模態上下文都會實質性地影響結果。最近關於Global-MMLU、CDEval、WorldValuesBench、CulturalBench、CULEMO、CulturalVQA、GIMMICK、DRISHTIKON、WorldCuisines、CARE、CLCA以及對基準設計和社區基礎評估的新批評的研究顯示，強大的多語言模型仍然可能壓平當地規範、誤解文化基礎的提示，並在資源較少或社區特定的環境中表現不佳。我們認為，該領域應該從將語言視為基準電子表格中的孤立行轉向建模交際生態系統：使用語言的機構、文本、翻譯管道、領域、模態和社區。在此基礎上，我們提出了一個以更豐富的上下文元數據、文化分層評估、參與式對齊、語言內變異和多模態社區意識設計為中心的文化基礎NLP研究議程。
2603.26013.author: Sina Bagheri Nezhad et.al.
